channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: For the first time in 8 years, a US Navy ship makes a port call in Cambodia, a top Chinese ally
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — A U.S. Navy warship arrived Monday in Cambodia, the first such visit in eight years to a nation that is China’s close ally in Southeast Asia. Cambodia's government has suggested the port call reflects an upgrade in often-strained relations.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean investigators push to summon impeached president as court set to determine his fate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol defied requests by investigative agencies to appear for questioning over his short-lived martial law decree, as the Constitutional Court began its first meeting Monday on Yoon's case to determine whether to formally unseat or
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipino woman who was on death row in Indonesia will return home after almost 15 years in prison
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipino woman who was on death row in Indonesia — and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 — was moved late Sunday to a female prison in Indonesia’s capital, from where she will be flown back to her home country.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI and NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Trump threatens mass deportations, Central America braces for an influx of vulnerable migrants
item.description: U.S. President-elect Donald Trump is set to take office in January with a promise of carrying out mass deportations, leaving Honduras and other Central American countries bracing for a potential influx of vulnerable migrants — a situation they are ill-prepared to handle.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel closes its embassy in Ireland
item.description: Israel announced that it would close its embassy in Ireland on Sunday after the European country recognized Palestine as a sovereign nation and other acts that Israel said promoted “antisemitic rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said Sunday it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorated over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials said new Israeli airstrikes killed over 46 people including several children.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolivia's divisive popular vote for senior judges offers lessons for Mexico
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning in Bolivia for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of the capital La Paz and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into off
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is 'several hundred,' top official says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The death toll in the French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is “several hundred” and may run into the thousands, the island’s top government official told a local broadcaster Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: West Africa regional bloc approves exit timeline for 3 coup-hit member states
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — West Africa’s regional bloc ECOWAS approved Sunday an exit timeline for three coup-hit nations after a nearly yearlong process of mediation to avert the unprecedented disintegration of the grouping.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump once stopped Erdogan attacks on US-backed Kurds in Syria, and has opportunity to do it again: Jack Keane
item.description: As the conflict expands between pro-Turkey Islamist forces and the pro-American Kurdish forces, there are growing efforts to rope in Ankara’s offensive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christians in Syria mark country's transformation with tears as UN envoy urges an end to sanctions
item.description: In churches across long-stifled Syria, Christians have marked the first Sunday services since Bashar Assad’s ouster in an air of transformation.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major international figures who died in 2024
item.description: Numerous key international figures died in 2024, ranging from Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny to Yahya Sinwar, the leader of Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US reiterates support for South Korea after impeachment of president
item.description: The United States reiterated its support for South Korea, an ally in East Asia, and vowed to advance mutual interests after the country’s president Yoon Suk Yeol was impeached by the parliament.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Filipino woman on Indonesia death row recalls a stunning last minute reprieve and 'miracle' transfer
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Filipino death row inmate Mary Jane Fiesta Veloso knelt to pray when officers came to take her to an execution site in May 2015, just a few feet away from her isolation cell on an Indonesian prison island, where a 13-member firing squad was waiting.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Capitol rioter who tried to join Russian army is sentenced to prison for probation violation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Dallas man who tried to fly overseas to join the Russian military and fight against Ukraine was sentenced on Friday to six months in prison for violating the terms of his probation for storming the U.S. Capitol four years ago. Kevin Loftus, a 56-year-old veteran of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia batters Ukraine power grid amid rising concern Putin could order ballistic missile attack this weekend
item.description: Ukraine's energy infrastructure was again pummeled in a "massive blow" by Russian missiles and drones on Friday, just days after the U.S. warned Russia might be planning another Oreshnik missile strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief warns of 'huge risk' in Ukraine talks without Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte expressed concern about the idea of conducting peace negotiations in the nearly three-year-long Russia-Ukraine war without Russian President Vladimir Putin "at the table," calling the move "risky.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau declares himself ‘proud feminist’ after lamenting Harris loss to Trump as setback for women
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday declared himself a "proud feminist" as he lamented Vice President Harris’ loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau: Harris loss a setback for women in politics
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday addressed Vice President Harris’s loss in the 2024 presidential election, calling it a setback for gender representation in politics, during a gala promoting women in politics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump has invited Xi Jinping to attend inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month in Washington, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation
